mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-HOUST momentum yields Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-rising months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion regime reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) during expansion phases, or positive signals coincide with negative market moves in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index exceeding 235 indicates persistent inflationary pressure that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 235, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll surprises coincide with negative market returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising retail sales signals inventory accumulation and demand overheating, triggering short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines QoQ and RSAFS rises MoM
failure_condition: The GDP-sales divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered cases, or interaction Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening regime reflects supply-side contraction and reduced economic activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or the signal produces positive market moves in > 60% of observations.
